questions.question: State on which side of the half-equation the electrons appear in an oxidation half-equation.
Write the oxidation half-equation for magnesium (Mg → Mg²⁺) and the reduction half-equation for copper ions (Cu²⁺ → Cu). Ensure each half-equation is balanced for both atoms and charge.

Write the oxidation half-equation for zinc (Zn → Zn²⁺) and the reduction half-equation for hydrogen ions (2H⁺ → H₂). Combine the two half-equations to give the overall ionic equation. State which species is the oxidising agent.
questions.context: Products of the overall reaction are zinc ions and hydrogen gas.

questions.question: State how water molecules (H₂O) and hydrogen ions (H⁺) are used when balancing oxygen atoms in half-equations written for reactions in acidic aqueous solution.
Write the oxidation half-equation for iodide ions (2I⁻ → I₂) and the reduction half-equation for iron(III) ions (Fe³⁺ → Fe²⁺). Ensure each half-equation is balanced for atoms and charge.

Write the reduction half-equation for the dichromate ion in acid solution (Cr₂O₇²⁻ → 2Cr³⁺), and the oxidation half-equation for sulfur dioxide (SO₂ → SO₄²⁻). Balance using H⁺ and H₂O. Combine the two half-equations to give the overall ionic equation.
Deduce the reducing agent in the reaction: 2Fe³⁺(aq) + 2I⁻(aq) → 2Fe²⁺(aq) + I₂(aq).
Describe the steps used to combine two half-equations into an overall ionic equation. Use the example of Fe → Fe³⁺ + 3e⁻ (oxidation) and Sn⁴⁺ + 2e⁻ → Sn²⁺ (reduction).

Write the oxidation half-equation for hydrogen sulfide (H₂S → S), the reduction half-equation for bromine (Br₂ → 2Br⁻), and combine them to give the overall ionic equation. Identify the oxidising agent and justify your choice using oxidation numbers.
State what is meant by a 'spectator ion' in an ionic equation.

Write the reduction half-equation for chlorine (Cl₂ → 2Cl⁻) in aqueous solution. State the number of electrons transferred per molecule of Cl₂ and identify whether chlorine is acting as an oxidising or reducing agent.

Tin(II) ions (Sn²⁺) are oxidised to Sn⁴⁺ by iron(III) ions (Fe³⁺), which are reduced to Fe²⁺. Write the two half-equations, combine them to give the overall ionic equation, and state which species acts as the oxidising agent and why.
State the name given to the ions that are excluded from an ionic equation because they do not change during the reaction.
Write the reduction half-equation for the manganate(VII) ion in acidic solution (MnO₄⁻ → Mn²⁺). Balance the half-equation using H⁺ and H₂O, and ensure it is balanced for charge.

In the reaction between acidified potassium manganate(VII) (KMnO₄) and iron(II) sulfate (FeSO₄), MnO₄⁻ is reduced to Mn²⁺ and Fe²⁺ is oxidised to Fe³⁺. Write the two half-equations, balance each for atoms and charge using H⁺ and H₂O where needed, and combine them to give the overall ionic equation.
questions.context: Reaction occurs in dilute sulfuric acid.
